name: University Of Va Community Cu
description_short: Virginia-based credit union established in 1954, serving 81,100+ members with $1.5B in assets. Offers checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and digital banking.
description_long:
University of Virginia Community Credit Union was established in 1954 and is headquartered in Charlottesville, Virginia. As the 13th largest credit union in Virginia, it has grown to serve over 81,100 members while stewards over $1.5 billion in assets. The institution operates as a state-chartered credit union focused on providing comprehensive financial solutions to Virginia residents.

The credit union offers a full spectrum of consumer and business financial products. Members can access checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), and share draft accounts. For borrowing needs, UOVCCU provides personal loans, auto loans, business loans, and multiple mortgage options including fixed-rate, adjustable-rate, and balloon/hybrid mortgages. Additional services include credit cards, wire transfers, bill pay, mobile payments, remote deposit capture, and e-statements.

UOVCCU distinguishes itself through its emphasis on member education and community support. The credit union offers financial counseling, financial education programs, financial workshops, and student scholarships. It maintains a dedicated team of over 300 employees and provides service through 1 main branch and 15 ATMs across the network. The institution supports both online banking and mobile apps for iOS and Android devices.

As a credit union rather than a commercial bank, UOVCCU operates on a member-owned cooperative model, typically offering more favorable rates and lower fees than traditional banks. However, with only one physical branch and 15 ATMs, members in distant areas may face accessibility challenges. The organization serves primarily Virginia residents under its state charter eligibility requirements. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Checking accounts (Share Draft accounts with 54,500+ members)
Savings accounts and regular savings products
Online banking with bill pay and remote deposit capture
pros: Member-owned credit union structure typically offers better rates and lower fees than commercial banks
Serves 81,100+ members with $1.5 billion in assets, indicating financial stability
Comprehensive product suite including mortgages, auto loans, business loans, and personal loans
Financial counseling and education programs available to help members improve financial literacy
Mobile banking app available for both iOS and Android with multiple digital services
Offers student scholarships, demonstrating community commitment beyond banking services
Online banking features including bill pay, remote deposit capture, and e-statements
cons: Limited physical presence with only 1 branch location in Charlottesville, restricting in-person access for distant members
State charter restricts membership to Virginia residents, limiting accessibility for out-of-state customers
No reviews available on the profiled website, making it difficult for prospective members to assess member satisfaction
Currently shows lobby and drive-thru as closed at time of data collection, raising questions about operational status
Small ATM network of only 15 machines compared to major banks with thousands of locations
best_for: Virginia residents seeking member-owned banking with potentially lower fees and better loan rates
Small business owners in Virginia needing business loans and personalized lending relationships
Individuals looking for comprehensive financial education and counseling services alongside banking
Members prioritizing community-focused financial institutions with scholarship and educational programs
diagnosis: University of Virginia Community Credit Union is best for Virginia residents seeking traditional banking services with a member-owned cooperative structure that typically offers competitive rates and personalized service. The main caveat is the single branch location in Charlottesville and state-charter membership restriction, which limits accessibility for those outside Virginia or in geographically distant areas.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
